title: Minimum wages

meta.description: National Minimum Wage and award minimum wages in Australia.

content:
From 1 July 2025, the National Minimum Wage is $24.95 per hour or $948.00 per week before tax. This is the adult minimum rate for employees not covered by an award or registered agreement. The National Minimum Wage is based on a 38-hour week for a full-time employee.

The 2024–25 Annual Wage Review increased the National Minimum Wage by 3.5%, effective from the first full pay period on or after 1 July 2025.

Employees covered by an award or registered agreement are entitled to the minimum pay rates set out in that award or agreement, which must be at least as much as the National Minimum Wage.

Award minimum wages also increased by 3.5% from 1 July 2025.

Junior employees, apprentices, trainees, and employees with a disability may have different minimum rates set by awards or the Fair Work Regulations.

Pay guides are available on the Fair Work website showing minimum pay rates for full-time, part-time and casual employees under each award, including all monetary allowances.

The Pay and Conditions Tool (PACT) can be used to calculate the correct pay rate for a specific employee.

Employees who are award and agreement free are entitled to the National Minimum Wage and the National Employment Standards (NES) only.

key_facts.applies_to: Employees not covered by an award or registered agreement
